title: Every requests is logged with default configuration
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I'm trying out Nomad with the Podman driver. Nomad will poll the container status every second, causing the podman logs to get very large, because the default log level is `info` when using the systemd service and this causes every request to be logged:

```
podman[99336]: @ - - [29/Oct/2022:13:27:22 +0800] "GET /v1.0.0/libpod/containers/acd2b091cb8a7069ca83d83cee215feff0e413ec34daafeddf516802f5cf5a40/stats?stream=false HTTP/1.1" 200 1398 "" "Go-http-client/1.1"
podman[99336]: @ - - [29/Oct/2022:13:27:23 +0800] "GET /v1.0.0/libpod/containers/acd2b091cb8a7069ca83d83cee215feff0e413ec34daafeddf516802f5cf5a40/stats?stream=false HTTP/1.1" 200 1398 "" "Go-http-client/1.1"
podman[99336]: @ - - [29/Oct/2022:13:27:24 +0800] "GET /v1.0.0/libpod/containers/acd2b091cb8a7069ca83d83cee215feff0e413ec34daafeddf516802f5cf5a40/stats?stream=false HTTP/1.1" 200 1398 "" "Go-http-client/1.1"
podman[99336]: @ - - [29/Oct/2022:13:27:25 +0800] "GET /v1.0.0/libpod/containers/acd2b091cb8a7069ca83d83cee215feff0e413ec34daafeddf516802f5cf5a40/stats?stream=false HTTP/1.1" 200 1398 "" "Go-http-client/1.1"
```

I would expect the default logging level to not include these requests in the log (either by changing the default logging level to `warn`, or by excluding these logs from the `info` level)

The default log level was set in https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/8390, but I think this qualifies as "log spam".

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install Nomad and the Nomad Podman driver.

2. Check the systemd journal

**Describe the results you received:**

Every instances causes 1 log entry per second

**Describe the results you expected:**

No log entries

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.3.0
API Version:  4.3.0
Go Version:   go1.19.2
Git Commit:   ad42af94903ce4f3c3cd0693e4e17e4286bf094b-dirty
Built:        Thu Oct 20 05:09:30 2022
```

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
Could you try to add a file

cat /etc/systemd/system/podman.service.d/local.conf
```
[Service]
Environment=LOGGING="--log-level=warn"
```
And se if it fixes your problem.  I think the thought would be the logging of the service should log each connection, but in your case Nomad is hammering the service, so it is filling the logs, dropping the service back to `warn` should fix this.

Since you are the first person to notice and complain about the setting, I am not sure this is a big deal in most situations.

@vrothberg WDYT?

It would probably be better to increase the timeout of the podman socket unit, instead of forcing it to stay up by probing it every second ?

~~Unless it actually _needs_ stats that often~~ Apparently it is a configuration value:

https://developer.hashicorp.com/nomad/docs/configuration/telemetry#collection_interval

I totally sympathize with the request.  There little value in logging such "keep alive" messages.  On the other hand, I see value in logging other requests; mostly when debugging.

But I am not married to the "info" log level, and am OK to drop it to warn.  @baude @Luap99 @mheon what do you think?

In my experience most web servers (nginx, apache, etc...) will also log all request by default. Therefore I think it makes sense for the podman service to do the same. 
But I don't have strong opinion on that, either way users can always change the log level as mentioned by @rhatdan.
I don't think we want to change this, so since the user can fix it, I am going to close this issue.
